name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) will be held in Vienna, Austria, from 6 to 10 July 2026. It offers a week‑long program of introductory and advanced AI courses, specialized tutorials, and networking opportunities for PhD students and young researchers, with special themes on Bilateral AI and Digital Humanism. The school also includes a rich social program, such as a reception at Vienna City Hall, to foster collaboration and community among participants.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
venue: Skeikampen conference hotel, near Oslo, Norway
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is organized by the Nordic Language Processing Laboratory and held at the Skeikampen conference hotel north of Oslo, Norway. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC consortium countries and doctoral students, and focuses on multilingual large language models, their evaluation, data curation, and open‑science practices. The program combines technical talks, hands‑on sessions, and field activities such as a ski‑friendly location and bus transfers from Oslo Airport.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: The Probabilistic AI School 2026 will be held in Vilnius, Lithuania from August 3‑7 2026. It aims to provide an inclusive educational environment covering state‑of‑the‑art machine‑learning and artificial‑intelligence topics for enthusiasts, students, academics and industry professionals. The school is co‑organized with the Artificial Intelligence Association of Lithuania and is part of the ongoing ProbAI School series.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a broad spectrum of computer‑vision topics, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme features daily oral talks and hands‑on sessions designed for students and early‑career researchers seeking advanced training in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
venue: Oxford ML School, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: OxML Summer 2026 is a programme offered by the Oxford ML School, combining in‑person sessions in Oxford with an online component. It provides three specialised tracks covering the latest AI theory and applications, taught by world‑renowned experts from leading academic and industry labs. Participants can customise their learning by selecting any combination of tracks to match their goals.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event organized by the Department for Robotics, Perception and Learning at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden. It brings together doctoral and postdoctoral researchers to discuss topics such as machine learning, computer vision, robotics, human‑robot interaction, planning and decision making through keynotes, industry talks, brainstorming sessions and student presentations. The program is held at the Happy Tammsvik resort and includes indoor and outdoor ice‑breaker activities, with all participant expenses covered by the school.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day program held in Edinburgh that brings together master’s‑level students and industry practitioners to explore advanced robotics topics. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials and industry talks covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI, safe AI and real‑world applications, with hands‑on experience using state‑of‑the‑art technology. Participants also benefit from networking opportunities and a special dinner, while the event is hosted at the National Robotarium, Heriot‑Watt University.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
venue: Hotel Dei Pini, Porto Conte (near Alghero), Sardinia, Italy
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies. It is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers, forensic examiners, police officers and other professionals, offering lectures by 20 leading experts and special keynote talks. The program includes hands‑on sessions, scholarship opportunities, and full‑board accommodation at Hotel Dei Pini in Porto Conte, Sardinia.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
description: AIces 2026 is the inaugural International School focusing on the cognitive, ethical, and societal dimensions of artificial intelligence. It is aimed at graduate and postgraduate students as well as industry practitioners who wish to engage with multidisciplinary discussions on AI foundations, challenges, and risks. The four‑day event in Porto–Maia, Portugal offers parallel courses, optional symposium and open‑debate sessions, live online participation, and recordings available for 45 days, culminating in a certificate of participation.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems is held at Umeå University in Sweden. It offers lectures, hands‑on multidisciplinary training, a poster session and networking activities focused on the ethical, legal and societal implications of AI and AS. The program targets students, faculty and professionals, with registration fees varying by participant type.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
description: The SoRAIM’27 Winter School focuses on social robotics, artificial intelligence, and multimedia, bringing together researchers from computer vision, speech and audio processing, affective computing, and human‑robot interaction. It is primarily aimed at PhD students and early‑career researchers, while also welcoming participants from industry and academia at all levels. Held in the scenic Vercors mountains near Grenoble, the programme combines lectures, hands‑on sessions, and informal networking in a relaxed mountain setting.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference includes a Winter School held from 11 to 15 January 2027. It focuses on recent advances in deep learning such as image classification, object detection, segmentation, time‑series prediction and speech recognition. The school takes place at UiT The Arctic University of Norway in Tromsø, providing researchers with opportunities to exchange ideas and foster collaborations.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program offering AI lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work. It targets a diverse audience that includes senior undergraduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, faculty members, and industry professionals, with more than 500 in‑person participants and thousands of livestream viewers. The school aims to provide fundamental AI knowledge, showcase state‑of‑the‑art research, and foster networking and collaboration among participants from around the world.

name: EEML Summer School
venue: Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School, Cetinje, Montenegro
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. The program features lectures and tutorials by leading researchers from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, University of Oxford, and many others, covering a wide range of machine‑learning topics. It is aimed at students and early‑career researchers who want to deepen their knowledge and network within the European AI community.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is a five‑day program offering keynotes, lectures and practical sessions on AI topics. It targets doctoral and outstanding master students, academics and practitioners, emphasizing diversity and inclusion, and will be held in Spain in September 2027. Participants can present posters, network with experts, and engage in cultural activities while exploring subjects such as computer vision, natural language processing, generative models and ethics.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day program held at the Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications in Rabat, Morocco. It targets master’s, Ph.D., post‑doc and early‑career researchers, offering theoretical lectures, hands‑on labs, and project mentorship on state‑of‑the‑art multimodal and generative AI techniques. Participants also benefit from networking events and interactions with leading AI experts from academia and industry.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing (AthNLP) Summer School 2026 will be held at the campus of NCSR Demokritos in Athens, Greece. The program brings together leading researchers from institutions such as RC Athena, NCSR Demokritos, Athens University of Economics and Business, Heriot‑Watt University, and ELLIS‑University of Manchester to deliver lectures, tutorials, and hands‑on sessions on modern NLP methods. It also supports the 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence (SETN 2026) that follows the school, providing participants with networking opportunities and exposure to cutting‑edge AI research.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school focuses on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning techniques such as GANs, flow‑based models, and self‑supervised learning. It is designed for STEM students with intermediate experience in AI/ML who want theoretical understanding and hands‑on experience for research or graduate study. The programme is offered as a residential or online course at Lady Margaret Hall, providing real‑world application projects and career‑relevant skills.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI is a comprehensive program that explores the intersection of artificial intelligence and scientific research. It offers hands‑on workshops, case studies, and discussions for PhD students, post‑doctoral researchers, and scientists, focusing on deep learning techniques applied to scientific data analysis, experimental design, and simulations. The event aims to equip participants with practical AI skills to accelerate scientific discovery across various domains.

name: LUT Summer School
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week intensive program held at LUT University’s Lappeenranta campus, offering bachelor’s and master’s level courses in sustainability, climate action, marketing, management and technology. The courses combine classroom teaching, online components and hands‑on workshops, and are aimed at international students seeking multidisciplinary knowledge and experience. Participants also enjoy a social programme set in the Finnish summer, including cultural activities and networking opportunities.
